verdict_reasoning: TGF-β1 is secreted but exists as the Large Latent Complex: the mature dimer remains non-covalently bound to LAP, which is covalently disulfide-tethered to LTBPs and to GARP/LRRC32 (or LRRC33) — single-pass TM proteins on the surface of Tregs, platelets, and other cells. This GARP–LAP–TGFβ1 complex is wash-resistant and presents latent TGFβ1 on the outer leaflet, where integrins (αVβ6, αVβ8) activate it in situ. Clinical antibody programs (e.g., anti-GARP/TGFβ1 complex antibodies) target this surface-displayed form. This is covalent/wash-resistant tethering to a TM partner acquired during secretion.
